commandsRun: cd infra && npx tsc -b && npx cdk synth (EXIT 0, 118 resources, 0 IAM wildcards)
rm -rf .turbo && DATABASE_URL=... AWS_ENDPOINT_URL=... pnpm test (41/41 cold)
implReport.notes: Acceptance suite COMPOSES existing cores (no reimplementation). Fixed cdk.json app -> node dist/bin/infra.js (synth was broken). Full §14 CdpStack: WAFv2 REGIONAL WebACL + stage association; REST API + RequestValidator + Model + UsagePlan + ApiKey + UsagePlanKey + authorizer; 13 Lambdas EACH own least-privilege role (0 Action:'*'/Resource:'*' across all 13 IAM::Policy via findResources); 2 FIFO main queues + FIFO DLQ RedrivePolicy + EventSourceMappings; SNS topic+lambda subscription; SES ConfigurationSet; S3+CloudFront OAC; 4 EventBridge rules; 21 CloudWatch alarms (SES 3%/5% bounce + 0.1% complaint, per-workspace CDP/Reputation custom metric, per-DLQ depth>0, per-function Errors, per-queue oldest-message-age); Secrets Manager/SSM refs no inline secrets. infra tsconfig exactOptionalPropertyTypes:false (CDK typing friction; business packages stay strict). scripts/ added as @cdp/scripts workspace pkg. Pen-test exercises RLS backstop (SET LOCAL ROLE cdp_app_test + setSessionClaims) AND in-code service-role scoping. SES/SNS/S3/SQS/DNS mocked; Postgres never mocked in acceptance/pen tiers. No real AWS deploy.
implReport.testSummary: Cold full pnpm test 41/41 packages green, no phase 2-13 regression. §18 acceptance gate 15 it() (one per §18 bullet, composing real ingest/processor/dispatcher/feedback/unsubscribe/onboarding/broadcast/campaign-runner/metering cores + service-api + RLS backstop) + penetration-isolation 9 it() (RLS backstop non-BYPASSRLS read+write block, system-admin RLS exception, ingest/processor service-role scoping forged workspace_id rejected, scopedQuery guard, local-api token isolation, audited cross-read) -> pnpm test:acceptance 24/24. CDK assertions+synth-smoke 11/11. health 5/5, dlq-replay 4/4.

invariants: Tenant isolation: every tenant-scoped row carries workspace_id NOT NULL; every query filters by workspace_id. Service-role Lambdas bypass RLS so they MUST scope by workspace_id in code; the admin app uses the user JWT so RLS applies.

workspace_id is NEVER from a client payload — it comes from the API-key/authorizer context or a trusted server-set field. The frontend sends the active-workspace claim via its JWT; the API resolves scope from the authorizer, never from a body field.

Role enforcement (§3A): owner/marketer/accounting are workspace-scoped; system-admin is the only cross-tenant role and every cross-tenant access is audit-logged. The UI shows only what the role permits; the API enforces it regardless of the UI.

All sends (broadcasts §9A and campaign actions §9B) go through the SAME outbox -> Dispatcher pipeline (§9) and pass suppression -> frequency-cap -> quiet-hours; sending is gated on workspace active/verified.

Idempotency: broadcasts dedupe per (broadcast_id, profile_id); the campaign runner must tolerate retries without double-advancing (optimistic checks / updated_at); image uploads + metering rollups are idempotent.

LOCAL-only: AWS (SES/SNS/S3/SQS) are MOCKED (aws-sdk-client-mock) or run on LocalStack; never hit real AWS. Do NOT mock Postgres in the integration tier — use the real local DB on localhost:5433. The editor must emit MJML, never hand-rolled email HTML.

Logic in pure injected functions; Lambda handlers stay thin. Reuse the prior harness (packages/db testutil: adminPool, hasDatabaseUrl, applyMigrations; runPlanInWorkspaceTx; SqlStatement {text,values}; @cdp/segments resolveAudience; @cdp/email; the dispatcher core).

Determinism: turbo test task is cache:false — keep it. Unique workspace UUIDs + file-local namespaces per integration file (events.event_id / ses_message_id are GLOBAL keys). No cross-file races. Frontend e2e (Playwright) must be deterministic (seeded data, stable selectors).
Scope discipline: implement exactly what the cited sections require for THIS phase — no features beyond the spec. Keep the cost model EXACTLY as §20 (direct usage + even split of fixed costs).
context.loop: Backend phase: pure unit tests + DB-integration against REAL local Postgres (localhost:5433); mock AWS (aws-sdk-client-mock) / LocalStack. No frontend.

criteria: The full §18 acceptance suite passes as one gate (tenant isolation, roles, ordering, no-loss, idempotency, segmentation, suppression scoping, reputation policing, broadcasts, campaigns, cost attribution).
CDK synth produces a WAF-protected REST API, least-privilege IAM per function, and the required alarms (verified by CDK assertions, no real deploy).
A DLQ replay script + /health exist; an isolation/role pen-test asserts no cross-workspace bleed via any path (system-admin access is audited).
scope: A consolidated §18 ACCEPTANCE test suite (the isolation + role + ordering + suppression + reputation + broadcast + campaign + cost criteria) runnable as one gate — the pass/fail merge gate.

CDK hardening (infra): WAF on the REST API stage, per-function least-privilege IAM, CloudWatch alarms (account + per-workspace reputation, DLQ depth, Lambda errors, SQS oldest-message age). Verify via CDK synth/assertions (no real deploy).
A DLQ runbook + replay script (scripts/) and a /health check; a lightweight load/perf sanity check of the ingest->processor path (local).
An isolation/role pen-test style test asserting no cross-workspace read/write is possible via any service path (incl. the system-admin audited exception).
phase.specSections: §13 (security/tenancy), §14 (IaC: WAF, least-privilege IAM, alarms), §16 (observability, DLQ runbook), §18 (full acceptance criteria), §17 phase 14
phase.title: Hardening (acceptance suite, WAF, DLQ runbook, load)

acceptanceMapping.coveredBy: spec18 'Tenant isolation' + pen-test paths 1-2 + RLS backstop

cdkResources: wafv2.CfnWebACL REGIONAL + WebACLAssociation to REST API stage
apigateway.RestApi + RequestValidator + Model
UsagePlan + ApiKey + UsagePlanKey throttle+quota
authorizer Lambda authorizer
lambda.Function per service each with own least-privilege role (.grant* scoped, no wildcards)
SQS FIFO ingest+dispatch + FIFO DLQ RedrivePolicy + EventSourceMappings
SNS topic + subscription -> feedback; SES CfnConfigurationSet
S3 bucket + CloudFront (minimal for synth)
EventBridge schedules batch-eval/soft-bounce/usage rollups
CloudWatch alarms: account reputation (3%/5%, 0.1%), per-workspace reputation, per-DLQ depth, per-fn errors, per-queue oldest-message age
Secrets Manager/SSM refs (no inline secrets), granted only to needers
implUnits: Fix infra/cdk.json app path node bin/infra.js -> node dist/bin/infra.js (synth broken today)
Author infra/lib/cdp-stack.ts full §14 graph, per-function least-privilege roles + .grant* helpers (TDD: assertions test first)
Build consolidated acceptance suite COMPOSING existing cores (no re-implementation), labeled it() per §18 bullet
add pnpm test:acceptance (vitest run acceptance), DATABASE_URL-gated
pen-test enumerate 5 paths + RLS backstop + system-admin audited exception (reuse scopedQuery/TEST_APP_ROLE/setSessionClaims)
real /health buildHealth(deps) db ping + DLQ probe, 200/503
scripts/dlq-replay.ts pure replayDlq (Receive->re-send preserving group+dedup ids->Delete; dry-run; intact-on-failure) + CLI + test
plan.notes: Heavy reuse: 3 existing integration suites + authorizer e2e already drive REAL cores against real PG proving most §18; acceptance suite COMPOSES same cores into ONE labeled gate 1:1 to §18 (§16A aggregate). Gaps to build: single-gate acceptance file + test:acceptance, cross-path pen-test, real /health (current is trivial stub), DLQ replay+runbook, entire CDK stack (empty CdpStack). CDK BUG FOUND: infra/cdk.json app 'node bin/infra.js' but tsc outDir dist/ -> synth fails MODULE_NOT_FOUND; fix to 'node dist/bin/infra.js'. Assertion tests use Template.fromStack (no deploy). CDK deps (aws-cdk-lib ^2.147, constructs, assertions) ALREADY installed in infra/node_modules. All gate hasDatabaseUrl, mock SES/SNS/S3, pen-test uses TEST_APP_ROLE non-BYPASSRLS + setSessionClaims. Strict TDD red->green. NO real AWS/deploy.

assertions: gated hasDatabaseUrl, adminPool+applyMigrations, file-local namespace, cleanup
each it() labeled with its §18 bullet (1:1 mapping)
Tenant isolation via real ingest+processor cores: WS_A/WS_B same external_id -> distinct profiles no bleed; A api-key only WS_A
RLS path: non-BYPASSRLS TEST_APP_ROLE + setSessionClaims WS_A -> 0 WS_B rows; service-role scoped via workspace_id $1
system-admin audited exception: is_platform_admin reads WS_B + writeAuditEntry one admin_audit_log row
Roles §3A: requireCapability/enforceRoute marketer/accounting/owner/system-admin boundaries
Ordering: created->progress and progress-first converge to one profile
No-loss: forced tx throw retried then succeeds, no event vanishes
Idempotency: repeat event_id -> unchanged, one events row
Segmentation: one entered/one exited never another workspace
Suppression scoping: A unsub != B; hard bounce in-workspace + global_hard_bounces
Reputation auto-suspend: offender suspended peer active, suspended refuses dispatch
Broadcasts send-once: dedupe per (broadcast_id,profile_id), retry no double-send, SES once
Campaigns advance trigger->wait->condition->action->exit idempotent runner
Cost sums to total: per-ws emails_sent reconciles messages_log, sum===direct+fixed to cent
Sending gated on verification: non-active refused no SES
enumerate 5 entry paths, WS_A actor can't read/write WS_B
ingest-by-key: resolveWorkspaceId(KEY_A) only WS_A, payload workspace_id ignored
local-api by token: profiles/segments/billing/admin return only WS_A, body workspace_id ignored
processor service-role: runPlanInWorkspaceTx WS_A writes only WS_A even on adminPool
dispatcher: outbox WS_A scopes lookups to WS_A
feedback: workspace from SES message tags only
RLS backstop non-BYPASSRLS WS_A -> 0 WS_B SELECT+UPDATE
system-admin is ONLY cross path + audited (one admin_audit_log); non-admin cross -> 0 rows no audit
no core accepts client-supplied workspace_id
Template.fromStack synth no error
WAFv2 WebACL REGIONAL + WebACLAssociation to REST API stage
RestApi 1 + RequestValidator + Model
UsagePlan + ThrottleSettings/QuotaSettings + ApiKey + UsagePlanKey
least-privilege: no IAM Action:'*' / Resource:'*'; each Lambda own role
SQS FIFO ingest+dispatch + FIFO DLQ RedrivePolicy maxReceiveCount
alarms: Reputation.BounceRate+ComplaintRate, per-DLQ depth>0, per-fn Errors, per-queue oldest-message age, per-workspace reputation custom metric
EventBridge schedules batch-eval/soft-bounce/usage rollups
SNS topic+subscription to feedback + SES ConfigurationSet
app synth doesn't throw; template has RestApi, WebACL, FIFO Queue, Alarm, Lambda
buildHealth ok:true checks db:ok when ping resolves
ok:false naming failing component -> 503 when db rejects
includes DLQ depth status (ok 0, warn >0)
replayDlq Receive -> re-send to source FIFO preserving MessageGroupId+MessageDeduplicationId -> Delete only after success
re-send failure leaves message intact; dry-run counts without moving
aws-sdk-client-mock, never real AWS

instructions: Clear the turbo cache and RUN the tests yourself (DATABASE_URL set) twice from cold, plus pnpm typecheck and pnpm lint. For UI phases, RUN the Playwright e2e in a real browser. Record real pass/fail. Confirm the integration tier RUNS (not skipped) and no regression in earlier phases.

Verify EACH acceptance criterion is covered by a passing, non-vacuous test: ["The full §18 acceptance suite passes as one gate (tenant isolation, roles, ordering, no-loss, idempotency, segmentation, suppression scoping, reputation policing, broadcasts, campaigns, cost attribution).","CDK synth produces a WAF-protected REST API, least-privilege IAM per function, and the required alarms (verified by CDK assertions, no real deploy).","A DLQ replay script + /health exist; an isolation/role pen-test asserts no cross-workspace bleed via any path (system-admin access is audited)."].

Highest-priority checks: (a) tenant isolation + role enforcement (server-side, not just UI-hidden); (b) all sends route through the Dispatcher guards; (c) idempotency (broadcast dedupe / no double-advance / idempotent rollups); (d) AWS mocked/LocalStack (no real AWS) yet asserted; (e) for UI, the flow actually works in a real browser against the real backend, not a stub.
If any integration test mocks Postgres, that is a FAIL. If a UI criterion is only unit-tested (no real browser), that is a FAIL.
Scope check: flag any feature beyond the cited sections; verify the §20 cost model is exact.
Score 0-100. Set testsPass and criteriaAllMet honestly. Give concrete prioritized recommendations.
prompt.outputFormat: JSON: { score: number, testsPass: boolean, criteriaAllMet: boolean, criteriaCoverage: [{criterion, met, evidence}], isolationOk: boolean, browserVerified: boolean, scopeCreep: string[], recommendations: string[], criticalIssues: string[] }
prompt.role: adversarial principal reviewer + QA engineer verifying spec-fidelity, tenant isolation, roles, and (for UI) real-browser behavior
prompt.task: Independently verify "Hardening (acceptance suite, WAF, DLQ runbook, load)" against its §18 criteria. Re-run the tests yourself (cold cache); do not trust the self-report.

title: Quality gate: Hardening (acceptance suite, WAF, DLQ runbook, load) (iteration 1)
